channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: US and Iran Trade Air Attacks for Second Day as Ceasefire Unravels
item.description: The United States and Iran exchanged air attacks for a second straight day, with President Trump vowing further strikes if Tehran does not immediately agree to a peace deal. The renewed fighting has cast fresh doubt over prospects for ending the conflict.

item.title: Israeli Airstrikes Kill 13 in Southern Lebanon as Hezbollah Campaign Continues
item.description: Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon killed at least 13 people on Wednesday, Lebanese security sources said, as Israel pressed its campaign against Hezbollah. The Iran-backed group claimed fresh attacks against Israeli forces in the south.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Lifts Five-Year Ban on Lebanese Imports in Support for Beirut Government
item.description: Saudi Arabia has lifted a roughly five-year ban on imports from Lebanon in a show of support for Beiruts embattled government, offering relief to businesses battered by years of conflict between Hezbollah and Israel.

item.title: US Becomes Worlds Top Oil Exporter, Upending Decades-Old Global Energy Order
item.description: The United States has become the worlds largest oil exporter, overtaking Saudi Arabia and Russia in a historic shift that tightens American companies grip on energy markets as Washingtons war with Iran reshapes global energy trade.

item.title: European Central Bank Set for Insurance Rate Hike as Iran War Fans Inflation
item.description: The European Central Bank is all but certain to raise interest rates on Thursday in the hope of nipping higher inflation in the bud before a surge in energy costs triggered by the Iran war spreads more broadly across the euro zone economy.

item.title: Iran and US exchange fire as hostilities escalate across Gulf
item.description: Iran retaliated against US strikes with attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, while the US military announced it completed additional strikes on multiple Iranian targets. Bahrain activated missile warning sirens and Kuwait temporarily closed its airspace.

item.title: Dollar wobbles as Middle East strikes unsettle markets
item.description: The US dollar fluctuated on Thursday as fresh US military strikes in the Middle East undermined sentiment, while a surge in May consumer inflation to a three-year high kept investors uneasy about the Federal Reserve outlook.

item.title: Trump praises Armenia PM election win despite Russian pressure
item.description: US President Donald Trump congratulated Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan for his partys decisive victory in weekend elections, a result that defied Russian efforts to influence the vote.

item.title: Ukraine strikes Russian-occupied Mariupol port
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck the Russian-occupied port of Mariupol, Kyiv said, the latest in a series of drone attacks on logistics across a critical stretch of Moscow-held southern Ukraine connecting Russia to Crimea.

item.title: Iran threatens to target any ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and threatened to target any ship transiting the strategic waterway following US military strikes. The move threatens to disrupt global oil shipments through the critical chokepoint.

item.title: US consumer inflation vaults above 4% as Iran war drives energy prices
item.description: US consumer inflation increased at its fastest pace in three years in May, boosted by surging energy prices amid the Middle East conflict, giving more ammunition for the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged into 2027.

item.title: EU fiscal watchdog criticises loosening budget rules for energy crisis
item.description: The European Fiscal Board warned that loosening EU budget rules in response to the energy crisis risks undermining fiscal discipline and long-term economic stability across the bloc.

item.title: Indian shares set to open lower on Middle East escalation and US inflation
item.description: Indian shares are set to open lower on Thursday as a fresh escalation in the Middle East conflict and a sharp jump in US inflation triggered risk-off sentiment across global equities.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV blesses Sagrada Familia on centenary of construction
item.description: Pope Leo XIV led a blessing at Antoni Gaudis Sagrada Familia in Barcelona, marking the centenary of the basilicas ongoing construction and celebrating its cultural significance.

item.title: Five EU countries call for united response to Trump drug price
item.description: Five EU member states are pushing for a coordinated European response to Trump administration pressure on pharmaceutical pricing, as tensions mount over transatlantic trade in healthcare.

item.title: UK-EU summit at risk as youth visa talks hit fresh obstacle
item.description: Negotiations over a UK-EU youth mobility scheme have stalled, threatening progress at the upcoming bilateral summit and complicating post-Brexit relations.

item.title: India calls in air force to secure exam papers after scandals spark protests
item.description: India has deployed air force personnel to secure examination papers after cheating scandals fueled nationwide protests and raised questions about the integrity of the education system.

item.title: US Strikes Multiple Targets in Iran as Trump Vows Further Escalation
item.description: The US military struck Iranian surveillance and air defence sites near the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran launched missiles and drones at US bases in Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain in retaliation.

item.title: Police Fire Water Cannons at Belfast Protesters as Unrest Flares
item.description: Police deployed water cannons against protesters in Belfast amid ongoing unrest following a stabbing incident, as tensions continue in Northern Ireland.

item.title: Trump White House UFC Fight Requires $60M and 7 Federal Agencies
item.description: A UFC event at the White House is drawing scrutiny over its $60 million cost and involvement of seven federal agencies, raising questions about use of government resources.

item.title: Japan Restarts Reactor at Worlds Largest Nuclear Plant Amid Energy Push
item.description: Japan has restarted a reactor at the worlds largest nuclear power plant as part of efforts to meet energy demands, though the nation faces challenges in managing radioactive waste.

item.title: Indonesia Sovereign Wealth Fund Tests Investor Appetite With Bond Sale
item.description: Indonesias sovereign wealth fund Danantara is launching a bond sale to test investor appetite following a recent selloff, as the country seeks to stabilize markets amid global uncertainty.

item.title: Chinese AI Police Tech Measures Vital Signs and Core Motivations
item.description: Chinese police are deploying AI technology designed to measure physical, psychological and emotional states during surveillance, raising concerns about privacy and civil liberties.

item.title: House Lawmakers Back 5-7% Military Pay Raise for 2027
item.description: House lawmakers have approved a 5-7% military pay raise for 2027, aiming to improve retention and recruitment as the armed forces face personnel challenges.

item.title: Indias Urban Boom Turns Cities Into Heat Traps as Climate Crisis Deepens
item.description: Rapid urbanization in India is transforming cities into heat traps, with poor planning and inadequate green space exacerbating the effects of climate change on vulnerable populations.
